title.purpose: Card, section, and modal headers — small and tight.

body.purpose: The workhorse 14px size for nearly all UI text and controls.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked sidebar dividers and micro-labels only.

data.purpose: Geist Mono with tabular figures for tables, IDs, timestamps, logs.

shadows.name: xs
shadows.purpose: Inputs and secondary buttons at rest.

shadows.name: md
shadows.purpose: Hover state for interactive cards; dropdowns.

shadows.name: xl
shadows.purpose: Modals and dialogs above a backdrop-blur scrim.

shadows.name: brand
shadows.purpose: The one cobalt-tinted glow — primary button on hover, used sparingly.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for control state transitions.

motion.name: duration-control
motion.purpose: Buttons, inputs, nav — quick, in-flow feedback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single high-emphasis action per view. Cobalt fill, tactile press, cobalt glow on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #fff; background: #3049c9; border: none; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(24 27 40 / 0.06), 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(24 27 40 / 0.08); cursor: pointer; user-select: none; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 150ms, transform 150ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2739a3; box-shadow: 0 6px 16px -4px rgb(48 73 201 / 0.35); } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #243284; transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #fff, 0 0 0 4px rgb(71 99 230 / 0.6); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action alongside a primary. White fill, hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #374151; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(24 27 40 / 0.06); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1), border-color 150ms; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f9fafb; border-color: #9ca3af; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #fff, 0 0 0 4px rgb(71 99 230 / 0.6); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Ring-based state token for job/system status. Tinted surface, colored text, inset ring, optional live dot.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 6px; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgb(5 150 105 / 0.2); } .ds-badge-success { background: #ecfdf5; color: #047857; } .ds-badge-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #10b981; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field with bound label. Cobalt focus glow, not a hard outline.
components.css: .ds-field { display: block; } .ds-field-label { display: block; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #374151; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #111827; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(24 27 40 / 0.06); transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 150ms; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9ca3af; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #4763e6; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(71 99 230 / 0.3); }

components.name: Card
components.description: The default raised working surface. Panel white on the tinted body, resting shadow, hairline border.
components.html:
Recent jobs

Job output and per-host results.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border: 1px solid rgb(229 231 235 / 0.8); border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(24 27 40 / 0.06), 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(24 27 40 / 0.08); overflow: hidden; } .ds-card-head { padding: 16px 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid #f3f4f6; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #111827; } .ds-card-body { padding: 24px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #1e2230; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: A navigation row from the dark command rail. Cobalt left marker and icon mark the active route.
components.html: DashboardJobs
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; width: 256px; padding: 12px; background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #111827, #111524); border-radius: 12px; } .ds-nav-item { position: relative; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 12px 8px 14px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #9ca3af; border-radius: 8px; text-decoration: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgb(255 255 255 / 0.04); color: #f3f4f6; } .ds-nav-active { background: rgb(255 255 255 / 0.06); color: #fff; } .ds-nav-active svg { color: #9db2fb; } .ds-nav-mark { position: absolute; left: 0; top: 6px; bottom: 6px; width: 2px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #6f8bf5; }

components.name: Workflow DAG
components.description: The signature surface: a dependency-free SVG layered directed graph shared by the workflow builder, detail, and live run views. In the run view, node fills are driven by real per-node status — the graph is the converged state. Shown here run-view colored: a converged node feeding a running node.
components.css: .ds-dag { display: inline-block; overflow: auto; background: #f9fafb; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 6px; } .ds-dag svg { display: block; }

narrative.overview: Praetor is the authoritative deck from which fleets are run. The interface reads like a command surface for serious infrastructure work: a deep cobalt identity, dense-but-composed layouts, and honest status everywhere. It is built for a team that spans the calm configuring hour and the tense incident minute in a single sitting. Every surface reinforces the platform's central promise: the state shown is the real, converged truth of what happened on the executors, never a hopeful projection. The system is deliberately quiet — character comes from restraint and craft, not decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Single authoritative accent: one deep cobalt ('Prussian'), used for action, selection, and state — never decoration.
Cool-tinted, textured light surface (#f4f5f8 with a faint dot-grid) — never flat clinical white.
One type family (Geist) in weights, with Geist Mono for data and logs; tabular figures wherever numbers matter.
Dense but legible: compact controls, calm status vocabulary, structural depth that stays quiet at rest.
Honest state above all — status is never ambiguous, and color is never the only signal.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Praetor has exactly one brand hue. Cobalt marks action, current selection, focus, and info — nothing else. Its authority comes from scarcity.

rules.name: The Honest Surface Rule
rules.body: The body is #f4f5f8 with its dot-grid, always. Flat clinical white (#fff, #f8fafc) is forbidden as a full-page background.

rules.name: The State-Never-By-Color-Alone Rule
rules.body: Job status is always carried by a label and, where live, a status dot — never hue alone.

rules.name: The Data-Viz Exception Rule
rules.body: Signal Teal and Dispatch Violet exist for the workflow DAG and nothing else. They are forbidden in general chrome — buttons, nav, badges, links, backgrounds. One accent everywhere; a scoped categorical palette only inside the data visualization.

rules.name: The Tabular Digit Rule
rules.body: Everywhere numbers matter — tables, metrics, logs — digits use tabular-nums so columns align.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Type sizes are a fixed rem scale, never fluid clamp(). Product density wants predictable sizes.

rules.name: The Cool-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Every shadow is tinted to rgb(24 27 40), never black. The cool tint keeps a dense UI from feeling clinical.

rules.name: The Lift-On-Intent Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are calm at rest. A card lifts only when clickable; a button presses on click. Motion is a response to intent, never ambient.

dos: Do keep cobalt scarce — action, selection, focus, and info only. One accent, earning authority through rarity.
Do keep the body on #f4f5f8 with its dot-grid, and keep working surfaces Panel White above it.
Do use Geist Mono with tabular-nums for all data, IDs, timestamps, and logs.
Do carry job status with a label (and live dot) as well as color — status must survive without hue.
Do tint every shadow to rgb(24 27 40) and let surfaces stay calm at rest, lifting only on genuine intent.
Do ship every interactive component with default, hover, focus-visible, active, and disabled states.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion and keep transitions state-driven and <=250ms.
donts: Don't reproduce the cramped, dated AWX / Ansible Tower look.
Don't drift toward the generic AI-SaaS template: no purple gradients, hero-metric card walls, decorative glassmorphism, or identical icon-card grids.
Don't go consumer or playful — no mascots, candy colors, big illustrations, or marketing flourish.
Don't ship sterile enterprise gray — no flat clinical #fff/#f8fafc backgrounds, dead grays, or zero-warmth surfaces.
Don't use background-clip:text gradient headings or colored border-left/right accent stripes.
Don't nest a card inside a card, or reach for a modal before exhausting inline alternatives.
Don't use fluid clamp() type or a second display typeface — one Geist family, fixed rem scale.
Don't let muted text drop below 4.5:1 contrast.
